{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Bank Marketer",
  "description": "Swift Consulting Limited - Our client, a reputable financial institution, is recruiting qualified and result-driven candidates&nbsp;to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Marketer

Job Location:&nbsp;Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Bank Marketer will be responsible for building and maintaining strong relationships with customers, promoting the organization&rsquo;s financial products and services, and driving customer acquisition to support business growth and revenue targets.


Major Responsibilities


	Develop and maintain strong relationships with new and existing customers.
	Promote the bank&rsquo;s products and services to meet customer financial needs.
	Identify and acquire new clients to grow the customer base.
	Provide professional advice on financial products such as savings, loans, investments, and digital banking solutions.
	Follow up on customer enquiries and resolve issues promptly.
	Meet and exceed assigned sales and performance targets.
	Ensure compliance with organizational policies and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare and submit periodic performance and activity reports.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Marketing, or a related field.
	1&ndash;3 years of experience in banking, sales, or customer relationship management (an added advantage).
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Good marketing and negotiation skills.
	Ability to meet targets and work under pressure.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and other digital tools.
	Professional appearance and attitude.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hireme@swiftconsulting.com.ng&nbsp;using &ldquo;Bank Marketer &ndash; Apapa&rdquo;&nbsp;as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;Only qualified candidates will be contacted.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-12",

Bank Marketer Job at Swift Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Swift Consulting Limited - Our client, a reputable financial institution, is recruiting qualified and result-driven candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Marketer

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Bank Marketer will be responsible for building and maintaining strong relationships with customers, promoting the organization’s financial products and services, and driving customer acquisition to support business growth and revenue targets.


Major Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and maintain strong relationships with new and existing customers.
	Promote the bank’s products and services to meet customer financial needs.
	Identify and acquire new clients to grow the customer base.
	Provide professional advice on financial products such as savings, loans, investments, and digital banking solutions.
	Follow up on customer enquiries and resolve issues promptly.
	Meet and exceed assigned sales and performance targets.
	Ensure compliance with organizational policies and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare and submit periodic performance and activity reports.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Marketing, or a related field.
	1–3 years of experience in banking, sales, or customer relationship management (an added advantage).
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Good marketing and negotiation skills.
	Ability to meet targets and work under pressure.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and other digital tools.
	Professional appearance and attitude.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Bank Marketer – Apapa” as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Assistant",
  "description": "Respected Solutions &amp; Investment is a leading loan, Investment and pawn company in Nigeria, providing quick and reliable loans against cars, gold, luxury watches, gadgets, and other valuables. Whether you require a loan against gold, luxury watch, jewelry, or even a against car, we provide competitive interest rates and easy repayment options. Our government approved and licensed services guarantee a secure and trustworthy borrowing experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Assistant
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikota, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Assist in recruitment processes including job postings, screening, and interview coordination
	Maintain and update employee records and HR databases
	Support onboarding and offboarding processes
	Assist in payroll preparation and attendance tracking
	Handle employee inquiries and provide HR-related support
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labor regulations
	Prepare HR reports and documentation as required
	Support performance management and employee engagement initiatives


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc. in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field
	2&ndash;3 years of relevant HR or administrative experience
	Basic knowledge of HR processes and labor laws
	Strong organizational and administrative skills
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools


Additional Requirement:


	Candidates must reside in close proximity to Ikota, Ajah (or be willing to relocate)


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;Hrmanager@respectedsolution.co&nbsp;using &ldquo;HR Assistant &ndash; Ikota Ajah&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

HR Assistant Job at Respected Solutions & Investment




        
    

        
        Respected Solutions & Investment is a leading loan, Investment and pawn company in Nigeria, providing quick and reliable loans against cars, gold, luxury watches, gadgets, and other valuables. Whether you require a loan against gold, luxury watch, jewelry, or even a against car, we provide competitive interest rates and easy repayment options. Our government approved and licensed services guarantee a secure and trustworthy borrowing experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Assistant
Job Location: Ikota, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in recruitment processes including job postings, screening, and interview coordination
	Maintain and update employee records and HR databases
	Support onboarding and offboarding processes
	Assist in payroll preparation and attendance tracking
	Handle employee inquiries and provide HR-related support
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labor regulations
	Prepare HR reports and documentation as required
	Support performance management and employee engagement initiatives


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc. in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field
	2–3 years of relevant HR or administrative experience
	Basic knowledge of HR processes and labor laws
	Strong organizational and administrative skills
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools


Additional Requirement:


	Candidates must reside in close proximity to Ikota, Ajah (or be willing to relocate)


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “HR Assistant – Ikota Ajah” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Representative (Igbo)",
  "description": "Mikano International Limited is recognized as a distinguished solution provider with an extensive portfolio of five divisions with over 27 years of operation in Nigeria; Power Generation, Steel Fabrication, Electrical Products and Solutions, Heavy Construction Equipment and Forklifts and Mikano Motors. Alongside with its sister companies Mikano Construction Limited, Mikano Foods Limited and Mikano Medical Limited.

Customer Service Representative (Igbo) Job at Mikano International Limited




        
    

        
        Mikano International Limited is recognized as a distinguished solution provider with an extensive portfolio of five divisions with over 27 years of operation in Nigeria; Power Generation, Steel Fabrication, Electrical Products and Solutions, Heavy Construction Equipment and Forklifts and Mikano Motors. Alongside with its sister companies Mikano Construction Limited, Mikano Foods Limited and Mikano Medical Limited.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Mikano International Limited was established in 1993 with the objective of carrying out quality and dependable power generating products. Its business included sales, servicing, maintenance, overhauling and rentals of Diesel and gas power generating sets. Since its establishment, it has been contributing to the development of the power sector in the Nigerian economy, and became a nation leader in our market as a one-stop-shop for Power Solution.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative (Igbo)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: FMCG

Job Summary


	Customer Service Representative is the front line of support for the company's customers.
	You'll be responsible for answering customer questions, resolving customer problems, and providing excellent customer service.


Requirement


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications.


Salary
N250,000 Net Monthly.

Note


	Proficiency in Igbo and previous work as a customer service representative are compulsory for this position.
	Only selected candidates will be contacted.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Information Technology Security Analyst",
  "description": "Koins Microfinance Bank Limited,&nbsp;a member of Primera Africa Group, is a fully licensed microfinance bank dedicated to providing innovative financial solutions to businesses and individuals. Our goal is to enhance financial inclusion by offering a comprehensive suite of services tailored to meet the diverse needs of our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Information Technology Security Analyst

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	The IT Security Analyst is responsible for protecting the organization&rsquo;s IT systems, networks, and data from cybersecurity threats. The role involves monitoring security tools, analyzing risks, responding to incidents, and ensuring compliance with established security frameworks and policies.


Responsibilities


	Monitor and analyze security alerts using SIEM, firewalls, and IDS/IPS tools
	Detect, investigate, and respond to cybersecurity incidents
	Conduct vulnerability assessments and support penetration testing
	Review system and network logs for suspicious activities
	Implement and maintain security controls and best practices
	Perform risk assessments and recommend mitigation strategies
	Ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory standards
	Collaborate with IT teams to secure infrastructure and applications
	Prepare reports on security incidents and risk posture
	Stay updated on emerging cybersecurity threats and trends


Education


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Computer Science, IT, Cybersecurity, or related field


Technical Skills:


	Knowledge of networking (TCP/IP, VPNs, firewalls)
	Experience with Windows and Linux systems
	Familiarity with SIEM, IDS/IPS, endpoint protection tools
	Understanding of cybersecurity threats, vulnerabilities, and risk management
	Basic scripting (Python, PowerShell, or Bash) is an advantage


Certifications (Preferred):


	CompTIA Security+
	Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH)
	CISSP (for experienced candidates)
	GIAC Security Essentials (GSEC).


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@koinsbank.com&nbsp;using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.",

Information Technology Security Analyst Job at Koins Microfinance Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Koins Microfinance Bank Limited, a member of Primera Africa Group, is a fully licensed microfinance bank dedicated to providing innovative financial solutions to businesses and individuals. Our goal is to enhance financial inclusion by offering a comprehensive suite of services tailored to meet the diverse needs of our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Information Technology Security Analyst

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The IT Security Analyst is responsible for protecting the organization’s IT systems, networks, and data from cybersecurity threats. The role involves monitoring security tools, analyzing risks, responding to incidents, and ensuring compliance with established security frameworks and policies.


Responsibilities


	Monitor and analyze security alerts using SIEM, firewalls, and IDS/IPS tools
	Detect, investigate, and respond to cybersecurity incidents
	Conduct vulnerability assessments and support penetration testing
	Review system and network logs for suspicious activities
	Implement and maintain security controls and best practices
	Perform risk assessments and recommend mitigation strategies
	Ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory standards
	Collaborate with IT teams to secure infrastructure and applications
	Prepare reports on security incidents and risk posture
	Stay updated on emerging cybersecurity threats and trends


Education


	Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Cybersecurity, or related field


Technical Skills:


	Knowledge of networking (TCP/IP, VPNs, firewalls)
	Experience with Windows and Linux systems
	Familiarity with SIEM, IDS/IPS, endpoint protection tools
	Understanding of cybersecurity threats, vulnerabilities, and risk management
	Basic scripting (Python, PowerShell, or Bash) is an advantage


Certifications (Preferred):


	CompTIA Security+
	Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH)
	CISSP (for experienced candidates)
	GIAC Security Essentials (GSEC).


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Legal & Compliance Officer",
  "description": "Koins Microfinance Bank Limited,&nbsp;a member of Primera Africa Group, is a fully licensed microfinance bank dedicated to providing innovative financial solutions to businesses and individuals. Our goal is to enhance financial inclusion by offering a comprehensive suite of services tailored to meet the diverse needs of our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal &amp; Compliance Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	The Legal Officer&nbsp;is responsible for providing end-to-end legal support to the organization, including drafting and negotiating contracts, advising on legal and regulatory risks, supporting data protection and corporate governance activities, managing external counsel, and assisting with dispute resolution.
	The role also involves contributing to policy development and ensuring overall compliance with applicable laws and internal standards.


Job Duties &amp; Responsibilities


	Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of agreements, including NDAs, Service Level Agreements, vendor/supplier contracts, and Data Processing Agreements (DPAs).
	Provide legal advisory support to business teams by identifying and mitigating risks in contracts and proposed commercial arrangements.
	Develop, maintain, and continuously improve contract templates, policies, and playbooks to ensure consistency and efficiency.
	Support data protection initiatives, including conducting Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) and ensuring compliance with applicable privacy regulations.
	Assist with corporate governance matters, including preparing board resolutions, powers of attorney, and maintaining statutory registers.
	Manage external legal counsel where required and monitor legal costs to ensure effective budget control.
	Support litigation, dispute resolution, and pre-litigation matters, while contributing to policy development and compliance advisory projects in collaboration with the Compliance team.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Law or related disciplines.


Relevant work experience:


	1-3 years of experience in product management role.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@koinsbank.com&nbsp;using the Job Position&nbsp; as the subject of the email.",

Legal & Compliance Officer Job at Koins Microfinance Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Koins Microfinance Bank Limited, a member of Primera Africa Group, is a fully licensed microfinance bank dedicated to providing innovative financial solutions to businesses and individuals. Our goal is to enhance financial inclusion by offering a comprehensive suite of services tailored to meet the diverse needs of our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal & Compliance Officer

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Legal Officer is responsible for providing end-to-end legal support to the organization, including drafting and negotiating contracts, advising on legal and regulatory risks, supporting data protection and corporate governance activities, managing external counsel, and assisting with dispute resolution.
	The role also involves contributing to policy development and ensuring overall compliance with applicable laws and internal standards.


Job Duties & Responsibilities


	Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of agreements, including NDAs, Service Level Agreements, vendor/supplier contracts, and Data Processing Agreements (DPAs).
	Provide legal advisory support to business teams by identifying and mitigating risks in contracts and proposed commercial arrangements.
	Develop, maintain, and continuously improve contract templates, policies, and playbooks to ensure consistency and efficiency.
	Support data protection initiatives, including conducting Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) and ensuring compliance with applicable privacy regulations.
	Assist with corporate governance matters, including preparing board resolutions, powers of attorney, and maintaining statutory registers.
	Manage external legal counsel where required and monitor legal costs to ensure effective budget control.
	Support litigation, dispute resolution, and pre-litigation matters, while contributing to policy development and compliance advisory projects in collaboration with the Compliance team.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s degree in Law or related disciplines.


Relevant work experience:


	1-3 years of experience in product management role.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the email.
